task_completion.description: 3D Slicer application is open with the MRHead volume loaded
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer interface is visible, showing the standard four-pane view (Red, Yellow, Green, 3D) with the brain MRI volume loaded.

task_completion.description: The axial (Red) view is navigated to show the lateral ventricles
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Red slice viewer displays a cross-section of the brain where the lateral ventricles (dark, butterfly-shaped fluid-filled cavities in the center) are clearly visible.

task_completion.description: The Screen Capture module or export dialog is opened
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Screen Capture' module panel is visible in the sidebar, or a 'Capture to File' context menu/dialog is open.

task_completion.description: Export settings are configured for the Axial/Red view only and PNG format
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Screen Capture settings show 'Master view: Red' (or similar indicating Axial view) and 'Mode: View' (ensuring only the slice is captured, not the full window). Format is set to PNG.

task_completion.description: The image is saved to the specified output path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog is visible with the path '/home/ga/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/ventricles_axial.png' entered, or the Screen Capture module shows this output path before capturing.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to take a screenshot
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal window is visible running commands like 'scrot', 'import', or 'gnome-screenshot'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Slicer GUI directly
integrity.visual_evidence: Mouse cursor or UI highlights are visible within the 3D Slicer application window.

integrity.description: The agent did not download an image from the internet
integrity.visual_evidence: No web browser is visible searching for or downloading brain MRI images.
